problem_statement: The flight search endpoint applies the max_price filter incorrectly: instead of returning flights priced at or below the requested maximum, it returns flights priced above the limit, indicating an inverted comparison in the search logic.
goal: Fix GET /flights/search so that max_price filters results to flights with price <= the provided value.
acceptance_criteria: GET /flights/search?max_price=200 returns only flights with price less than or equal to 200.
Flights priced above max_price are excluded from the results.
Existing search behavior for other filters remains unchanged.
constraints: Limit changes to the backend flight search implementation unless a shared schema/API contract issue is discovered.
Keep the fix as small and localized as possible.
unknowns: Whether the filter is implemented directly in the service layer or delegated through repository/query construction.
Whether any tests currently cover max_price behavior or need to be added alongside the fix.
repo_summary: Backend-focused repository with a flight search API. Relevant candidate area is backend/app/services/flight_service.py, with nearby route/schema/model files for the flights domain.

targeted_symbols.reason: This is the affected implementation area explicitly called out in the issue; the max_price comparison is likely inverted here.
Useful to confirm request parameter names and how max_price is passed into the service, though the bug likely lives in the service layer.

localization_rationale: The issue description precisely identifies the affected behavior: max_price returns flights above the limit, which strongly suggests a reversed comparison operator.
The issue explicitly names backend/app/services/flight_service.py (search_flights) as the affected file, making this the smallest likely implementation area.
The route file is included only as a secondary check for parameter wiring; it is less likely to contain the bug than the service search logic.
